chunks.content:
Article: Questions Thoughts About Official Eva Music
[]: Cruel Angel Thesis Clip             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 08, 2006 6:19 am Hello everyone, I'm Legend of the Past. I hope to be active and enjoy things here, and be helpful in discussions to come. So, onto discussion. I've been watching an high quality version of Cruel Angel Thesis on YouTube, and saw all the things flashing there-Embryonic and Mature Adam, young Gendo Ikari, Rei sitting naked with her knees covering her torso next to what looks like a glass of water, SEELE logo, a part of the earth burning, etc etc etc... One intresting flash appears right at the last five seconds, where you see several shots of Shinji face, and then you see him shielding himself from some kind of light. It's really hard to focus on them in regular speed, because the moment you try to focus on one shot, you miss another two. That felt reminiscent of hidden messanging and use of flashes, a sort of hypnosis. My guess is that the subconsious does realize and catch those images, so that if you see the video in good quality, you can see things like Embryonic Adam and feel like you've seen it before. It's a try to make you feel like a part of the show. I'm sure you know a similar thing happans with 'Komm, Susser Todd'. The song's clapping makes you want to clap along, and the repeating of the chorus at the end makes you sing along. The chorus is short and very catchy, built of two five word sentences ('It all comes tumbeling down' and 'I just keep letting me down'), but the repeat chorus cuts it down to shorter sentences ('Letting me down' and 'Tumbeling down'). That makes the song all the more catchy and seductive, makes you want to sing and clap along. KST was composed by Anno himself, and is the song that plays during Third Impact. By singing along and clapping along, you 'give up your individualty' and become instead of a listener another singer, you join the group. Also, the singing and clapping makes you feel like you can't always tell your voice from the singers, and like Rei says, the Sea of LCL is an ambigious world, where you can't tell where you begin and others end. Anno's a fricking genius, even if the methods he makes you

Article: Questions Thoughts About Official Eva Music
 tell your voice from the singers, and like Rei says, the Sea of LCL is an ambigious world, where you can't tell where you begin and others end. Anno's a fricking genius, even if the methods he makes you become part of the show can really frighten you the moment you realize.

The song's clapping makes you want to clap along, and the repeating of the chorus at the end makes you sing along. The chorus is short and very catchy, built of two five word sentences ('It all comes tumbeling down' and 'I just keep letting me down'), but the repeat chorus cuts it down to shorter sentences ('Letting me down'

Article: Questions Thoughts About Official Eva Music
 along. The chorus is short and very catchy, built of two five word sentences ('It all comes tumbeling down' and 'I just keep letting me down'), but the repeat chorus cuts it down to shorter sentences ('Letting me down' and 'Tumbeling down'). That makes the song all the more catchy and seductive, makes you want to sing and clap along. KST was composed by Anno himself, and is the song that plays during Third Impact. By singing along and clapping along, you 'give up your individualty' and become instead of a listener another singer, you join the group. Also, the singing and clapping makes you feel like you can't always tell your voice from the singers, and like Rei says, the Sea of LCL is an ambigious world, where you can't tell where you begin and others end. Anno's a fricking genius, even if the methods he makes you become part of the show can really frighten you the moment you realize.

[]: What is the original source for the song "Thanatos&quot             Quote Post by apu » Sat May 15, 2010 1:19 pm I love the track Thanatos from the OST (Volume II), but recently on youtube I found this: [url]http://youtube.com/watch?v=sAbMeMdpiys[/url]. which has a very similar tune being used in an X-Files episode. Does anyone what the original source of the melody is? It seems too similar to just be a coincidence... [P.S. this is my very first evageeks post! ]

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat May 15, 2010 1:58 pm It could be that this piece was written before Evangelion and, when Evangelion was made, re-recorded the piece for the show. Where to get the original? Well, I'm not sure how...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat May 15, 2010 2:09 pm The music in question was played in an episode of season 4 of X files. The season began running October 4, 1996. The "Thantos" theme in Evangelion was first released as a single October 25, 1995, necking ahead of X files out by almost a year. Also, the X files theme only seems to be playing only part the harmony of

Article: Questions Thoughts About Official Eva Music
Thantos" theme in Evangelion was first released as a single October 25, 1995, necking ahead of X files out by almost a year. Also, the X files theme only seems to be playing only part the harmony of the song. Never is the harmony every played in it's entirety and the melody heard in Evangelion is never heard at all in the X files series. It's like playing the guitar chords of a Beach Boys song. It doesn't matter which one because they all sound exactly like each other.

[]:             Quote Post by apu » Sat May 15, 2010 2:13 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever: you rock! It never even occurred to me that Eva might have been first...I guess because I remember watching X-Files when it was on tv back then, but I only watched Eva for the first time this month.

[]: Favorite version of Cruel Angel's Thesis             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Fri Aug 27, 2010 1:47 am As many of you probably know, there are countless versions of NGE's famous opening theme, Cruel Angel's Thesis.(Zankoku na Tenshi no THESIS) So which version of the opening is your favorite? This could either be by a well-known singer or a cover on YouTube. Mine would have to be the acapella cover by Eri Kawai. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iuwko1ycNEc I felt that the singer really captured the transcendent tone of the lyrics with her voice and pacing. May she continue singing like that in the afterlife.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Fri Aug 27, 2010 2:06 am Well, I'd tell the truth and say 'the original' but that'd be borring so I'll go with the Ayanami Mix. Edit: The actual name is [A.D. 2001 mix]
